findings:
Today's tape lands a **direct 5/5 match** on three of the most strategically loaded predictions from the trailing week. The Apr 27 OpenAI–Microsoft AGI-clause unwind plus the AWS-Anthropic Trainium/Graviton silicon-level deepening collapse the Apr 25 "training oligopoly = 3 clouds" prediction and the Apr 26 "compute-stack lock-in" prediction into the same evening's news cycle — with one important inversion: the lock-in is real, but it is **multi-cloud-distribution-of-each-frontier-lab** rather than the original "each cloud locks one lab" framing in the Apr 21 5-pole regime call. Microsoft's $135B / 27% economic stake stays put while Azure exclusivity falls; the 5-pole map has to be redrawn as a many-to-many graph weighted by silicon-level co-engineering (Trainium-Anthropic, Graviton-Meta, Azure-OpenAI legacy, AWS-OpenAI new).

The AI-security thesis line — Apr 21 GGUF-supply-chain, Apr 26 inference-servers-as-primitive-supply-chain, Apr 27 AI-Infra CVE class — gets reinforced rather than resolved. SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) **remains unpatched into day 10+** despite a public PoC and a copy-paste class spreading to vLLM (CVE-2025-61620) and LMDeploy (CVE-2026-33626). The Totolink A8000RU CVE quad (CVE-2026-7153 / 7154 / 7156 / 7202, all 9.8) plus the Cisco ISE unauth-RCE advisory landing in the same 48h window show the underlying CVE-publication tempo NIST flagged is still rising. CISA's last KEV add was Apr 24 (4-entry batch: Samsung MagicINFO 9 / SimpleHelp x2 / D-Link DIR-823X), and no dedicated "AI-Infra" sub-catalog has shipped yet — the Q3/Q4 2026 forecast remains on schedule but unconfirmed.

The Apr 27 prediction on Mag 7 prints resetting AI-capex ROI is **already partially self-fulfilling** — today's news flags the per-token-margin-gating thesis as the central narrative under "Why This Week Could Be Huge For AI Stocks" and "Can Big Tech Justify $645 Billion In AI Spending?" headlines. The actual prints (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META Apr 29 + AAPL Apr 30) sit just outside today's window, so this prediction is in the "narrative confirmed, outcome pending 24-72h" state.

Two predictions show low-relevance status today: the Apr 21 "proprietary re-expansion = closed weights wins" (signal mixed by talkie-1930 open release alongside Qwen3.6-Max-Preview API-only) and the Apr 23 "third-party-vendor boundary + URL-naming entropy" (no fresh contractor-incident or URL-randomization mandate in today's window). The Apr 22 "OAuth trust scope" thesis sits at 2 — Google/Forcepoint IPI evidence reinforces the underlying risk vector but no SSO scope-category carve-out happened today.

The Apr 23 "Agent Control Plane" and Apr 26 "consumer-GPU coding agent displaces cloud-only API" predictions both pick up new evidence from today's standing-slot adds: **Multica v0.2.16 (Apr 24)** lands as an open-source vendor-neutral managed-agents orchestrator sitting in the same orchestration layer as AgentCore Managed Harness and Claude Managed Agents — strengthening the "Agent Control Plane" thesis with an OSS competitor; and **Unsloth v0.1.37-beta (Apr 23) + v0.1.36-beta** (Gemma-4-E2B 8 GB VRAM training ~1.5x faster / ~60% less VRAM than FA2) pairs with Bonsai-8B as the on-device fine-tuning baseline, materially strengthening the consumer-GPU thesis enough to bump that row to 4.

The dormant-pool entry **20260420-3 "Headless Everything architecture standardizes"** is not yet due for periodic ping (next 2026-05-11). Layer 1 keyword scan fired on the signal token "Simon Willison" — but the matching context is the OpenAI/Microsoft AGI-clause history primer plus VibeVoice and talkie-1930 model launches, none of which speak to headless-vs-GUI-driving agent architecture. Layer 2 semantic scan against today's headlines and subheadings produced no plausible match. Honest revival relevance: 1.
relation_to_my_preds:
**On "malicious local LLMs functioning like malware + zero-trust as fundamental safeguard":** Today's report carries strong signal on the zero-trust half of the thesis, weaker direct signal on the local-LLM-as-malware half. The zero-trust evidence stack is unusually dense: Okta for AI Agents GA is **2 days out (Apr 30)** with the exact "design system paths AI alone cannot access" pattern — Agent Discovery & Governance, Agent Gateway as a central MCP control plane, credential vaulting + auto-rotation, runtime enforcement via universal logout. Microsoft's "Zero Trust for AI" pillar (RSAC 2026 carry-forward), Cisco's "action control" framing for the agentic AI workforce, and the cross-vendor CrowdStrike/Splunk consensus all reinforce zero-trust-for-agents as the assumed enterprise baseline. The behavior-monitoring half (Google + Forcepoint IPI in-the-wild reports, OpenAI's public concession that prompt-injection is permanent) tracks the secondary half of your prediction — solutions claiming to monitor and manage AI behavior **are getting attention right now** (Forcepoint's 10 fresh IPI payloads gets prominent coverage), but the underlying message from OpenAI is essentially that monitoring is necessary-but-not-sufficient and that the threat class is structural. That maps almost exactly onto the "short-term attention but doesn't overturn the bypass assumption" caveat in your prediction.

**On "cloud APIs for advanced tasks + local LLMs for daily operations, driven by SaaS price rises":** Today's tape has the demand-and-supply-side signals but does not yet show explicit consumer-facing AI-SaaS price hikes. Demand side: the Apr 27 contract restructurings explicitly route AI-lab compute through hyperscaler-owned silicon (AWS Trainium/Graviton with Anthropic; Meta Graviton-at-scale CPUs); Mag 7 super-week aggregates ~$700B 2026 capex with the per-token-margin-gating thesis hardening. Supply side: DeepSeek V4-Pro at $1.74/$3.48 per M tokens with 1M context, DeepSeek V4-Flash 75%-off (carry-forward), PrismML Bonsai-8B 1-bit getting 44 tok/s on iPhone 17 Pro Max / 131 tok/s on M4 Pro / 368 tok/s on RTX 4090, Qwen3.6-27B as Apache-2.0 dense option. The local-vs-cloud bifurcation is being built; the explicit price-rise-as-trigger event that you call out has not landed today, but the **per-token-margin-gating disclosure as upcoming Mag 7 signal** is essentially a leading indicator of exactly the pricing-structure shift you predicted.

**On "RL/LLM-based forecasting performance improvement becomes widespread":** No direct signal in today's report. Adjacent-but-not-confirming items: AgentCore Managed Harness's mid-session model-swap and per-session microVM (a forecasting-eval-friendly substrate), and Anthropic's Rate Limits API + Claude Managed Agents public beta (programmable harness for batch evals). The thesis remains plausible but unaddressed in this 48h window.
